seo.title: Export Telegram chats — in your browser, nothing stored on a server | Telegram Exporter
seo.shareTitle: Export Telegram chats — in your browser, nothing stored on a server
seo.description: Export your Telegram conversations to a zip file. Everything runs inside your browser and nothing is stored on a server — your phone number, login code and messages are never sent anywhere else. Nothing to install.
seo.shareDescription: Export Telegram chats to a zip file, right in your browser. Nothing is stored on a server.

app.title: Telegram Exporter
app.tagline: Telegram chat backup with no server in between

landing.title: Save your whole Telegram history to your own computer
landing.lede: It runs right in your browser. Nothing to install, no account to create. Your phone number and your messages never pass through a server of ours — because there is no such server to pass through.

screenshots.title: See the actual screens
screenshots.body: Swipe through. From signing in to downloading, this is exactly what you will see.

why.title: How this differs from tools you install

install.title: Nothing to install
install.body: No installing Python or Node, no opening a terminal. If you have a browser it works — Windows, macOS, Android and iPhone alike.

server.title: No server in between
server.body: Your browser connects to Telegram directly. There is simply no middle server for your phone number, login code or messages to pass through.

output.title: Output a person can read
output.body: Unzip it and you get an HTML document that looks like the conversation itself. It opens without an internet connection, and without this tool.

zip.title: This is what you get
zip.html: Reads like the conversation itself. Open this one first
zip.jsonl: One message per line. The raw form, for machines to read again
zip.meta: Chat info, message count, export timestamp

one.title: Sign in with Telegram
one.body: Enter your phone number and the login code Telegram sends you.

two.title: Pick a chat and a date range
two.body: Jump to a date on the calendar and take only the stretch you need.

three.title: Download the zip
three.body: You see the progress, and you can stop at any time.

verify.title: Don't take our word for it
verify.body: Your browser locks this page so that it cannot connect to any address other than Telegram. Not even the author of this page can break that rule.
verify.bodyAnalytics: Your browser locks this page so that it cannot connect to any address other than Telegram and Google Analytics, which counts visitors. Not even the author of this page can break that rule. Your messages, phone number and login code are not sent to Google either.
verify.devtools: Press F12 and open the Network tab to see every address this page actually connects to.
verify.source: The full source code is public.

final.title: Back up your chats now
final.body: No sign-up, no install, no payment.

trust.purpose: This page exists to let you back up your Telegram conversations, simply.
trust.lede: It saves your chats as a zip file. Everything happens inside your browser — the program you use to view the web, such as Chrome or Safari.

noServer.title: Nothing passes through a server of ours
noServer.body: Most websites send what you type to their own servers. This page has no such server at all. Your browser talks to Telegram directly.

credentials.title: Your phone number and login code go only to Telegram
credentials.body: The values needed to sign in are used for nothing but the request to Telegram. There is no path by which we could see them.

download.title: Your messages become a file straight away
download.body: Messages are shown on screen, and when you export, they are zipped and saved to your own computer. Nothing is uploaded anywhere.

verify.title: How to check for yourself
verify.csp: This page is locked so that it cannot connect to any address other than Telegram (wss://*.web.telegram.org). Your browser enforces that rule, so not even the author of this page can break it.
verify.devtools: Press F12 (⌥⌘I on a Mac) and open the Network tab to see every address this page actually connects to.
verify.source: The full source code is public.
verify.peek: See for yourself that this page cannot connect anywhere else.
verify.cspAnalytics: This page cannot connect anywhere except Telegram (wss://*.web.telegram.org) and Google Analytics for visit counts — nothing else. The browser enforces this rule, so the site cannot change it.

stored.title: What stays in this browser
stored.lede: No personal information and no message content is kept. Only material that looks the same to everyone, plus the value that keeps you signed in. This table is the complete list.
stored.sessionUntil: When you close the tab · after {{minutes}} minutes idle · when you sign out
stored.sessionNote: Kept only if you tick “stay signed in”, so that a refresh doesn't make you log in again. Other tabs and windows cannot see it.
stored.stickerUntil: When you sign out · oldest first once past 20MB
stored.stickerNote: Stickers look the same to everyone on Telegram, so they aren't personal information. Only the picture is kept — never who sent it or when. They are cached because fetching them again each time is slow.
stored.peek: Just two things: your signed-in state and sticker images. No message content.

never.title: What is never kept
never.body: Conversations, photos and attachments, phone number, contacts, login code. None of it is stored, and none of it goes anywhere except to Telegram.

analytics.title: Visit statistics connect to Google
analytics.peek: Visit counts only. No conversations or phone numbers.
analytics.lede: This site uses Google Analytics to count visitors.
analytics.network: That means this page also connects to Google, not only Telegram. It loads the analytics script from googletagmanager.com and sends visit records to google-analytics.com. This is the only outbound connection besides Telegram, and you can see it in the Network tab of your developer tools.
analytics.sent: What is sent: that the landing page was opened, and which language.
analytics.notSent: What is never sent: your conversations, phone number, login code, or which chats you opened. Chat URLs contain the chat id, so those addresses are deliberately withheld.
analytics.block: Blocking it with an ad blocker or your browser's tracking protection does not break this tool.
analytics.selfHost: If you do not want even this, download the code and run it on your own machine. The analytics setting is supplied only when this site is deployed, so a copy you build yourself contains no analytics at all — the connection to Google is simply not in the code.

ads.title: Ads are shown
ads.peek: Google AdSense ads are present. Unrelated to your conversations.
ads.body: This site carries Google AdSense ads. It therefore also connects to Google's ad network (googlesyndication.com, doubleclick.net), and ads run inside a separate frame.
ads.isolation: Ad code cannot read anything on this page. Your conversations, phone number and login code are never shared with advertisers. No ads are placed on the sign-in or conversation screens.
ads.selfHost: To use it without ads, download the code and run it on your own machine. The ad setting is supplied only when this site is deployed, so a copy you build yourself contains no ad code at all.

device.title: Speed depends on the device you're using
device.body: Having no server in the middle also means the PC or phone you're on right now does all the fetching and compressing itself. So the same chat can take a different amount of time on a different device.
device.bigChat: For chats with a great many messages, use a PC. Phones have less memory to spare, so an export can be slow or stop partway.
device.keepOpen: Keep this tab open while exporting. If the screen turns off or you switch to another app, the browser will slow the work down or pause it.
device.rate: Telegram limits how fast requests can be made, so a large chat means waiting even on a fast device. It hasn't stalled.
device.peek: With no server in the middle, how long it takes depends on your own PC or phone.

warning.title: This tool asks for your login code
warning.principle: Telegram tells you never to share your login code with anyone. That advice is right. Don't make an exception for this site — be just as suspicious of it.
warning.more: Why it needs this, and what stays
warning.why: That said, this tool signs in to Telegram as you in order to fetch your messages. Without your phone number and login code there is nothing it can do. Those values are used only for the request to Telegram and are never stored anywhere.
warning.storage: After you sign in, the only thing kept is a single value that holds the connection open, and it lives only inside the tab you're looking at — close the tab and it's gone.
warning.selfHost: If you're still unconvinced, don't take anyone's word for it. Read the code, or download it and run it on your own machine. You'll get exactly this screen, running code you checked yourself.

credentials.title: Get started
credentials.modeSharedHint: Uses the app key bundled with this site. You only need your phone number.
credentials.modeCustomHint: Use a key you registered yourself at my.telegram.org.

guide.title: How to get an api_id
guide.step1: Enter your phone number at my.telegram.org. The confirmation code arrives in the Telegram app, not by SMS — keep Telegram open on another device.
guide.step2: Once signed in, open API development tools and fill it in like this. Use Latin letters and digits only — anything else fails without telling you why.
guide.step3: Submitting gives you an api_id and an api_hash. Paste both into the fields above.
guide.shortNameNote: 5–32 characters, letters and digits only. It has to be globally unique, and a name already taken shows up as a bare ERROR — try adding a number.

trouble.title: If all you get is ERROR
trouble.intro: That page never tells you why it failed. Check these, most likely first.
trouble.reload: Reload the page first. The app is often created even though ERROR appeared.
trouble.vpn: Turn off your VPN or proxy. If your IP's country differs from your phone number's country, Telegram blocks it. This is the most common cause.
trouble.extensions: Retry in a private window with every extension disabled. Ad blockers stop the form submission itself.
trouble.shortName: Try a different short name. A name collision also shows up as a bare ERROR.
trouble.spambot: Check for account restrictions. New or flagged accounts cannot create apps. Message @SpamBot in Telegram to find out.
trouble.wait: Wait 15–60 minutes and try once. Repeated attempts are rate limited on their own.

credentials.remember: Remembers your api_id in this browser. It is an app identifier, not account access, so storing it is safe.

expired.title: Your session expired
expired.body: You hadn't used this tab for over {{minutes}} minutes, so the sign-in details stored in this browser were cleared. Your account is untouched — just sign in again.

invalid.title: Please sign in again
invalid.body: The stored sign-in details could not be resumed. The session may have been terminated from another device, or Telegram expired it.

auth.rateLimit: Requesting the code several times in a short span makes Telegram temporarily limit this number, sometimes for up to a day. If the code is slow to arrive, wait a little before asking again.
auth.keepSignedIn: Stay signed in on this tab
auth.keepSignedInHint: Reloading won't ask you to sign in again. Closing the tab or leaving it unused for {{minutes}} minutes clears it. Turn this off on a shared computer.

phone.title: Phone number
phone.willSend: The code will be sent to:
phone.countrySearch: Search by name, code or number
phone.unverified: This does not match the number format for that country. If it is not a typo, you can continue anyway.

code.titleApp: Code sent to your Telegram app
code.hint: Enter the 5-digit code that arrived in Telegram on another device.

password.title: Two-step verification password
password.hint: Enter the two-step verification password set on your account.

auth.confirmSignOutBody: This also ends the session on your Telegram account. Using the tool again means requesting a new login code.

download.title: Run it yourself
download.hint: Open it on your own computer, not through this site.
download.body: Use it without going through this site. Unpack the zip and open index.html. The downloaded copy ships without analytics and cannot reach anything other than Telegram.
download.csp: The downloaded copy carries this rule and the browser enforces it. Nothing outside this list can be reached.

dialogs.title: Chats
dialogs.subtitle: Pick the chats you want to back up.

messages.dayEmpty: No messages on {{date}}. Showing from {{landed}}, the next day with messages.

actionKind.invitetogroupcall: invited someone to a voice chat

calendar.dayEmpty: No messages on {{date}}. Pick another day.

export.title: Export this chat
export.description: Downloads messages as a zip. Compression happens in your browser and no server is involved.
export.rangeHint: For very old chats, exporting in slices is safer.
export.wholeHistoryHint: Ignores the dates and takes everything from the start. Large chats can take a very long time because of Telegram's rate limits.
export.waiting: Waiting out a Telegram rate limit. It hasn't stalled — please keep this tab open.
export.savedPicked: It's in the location you picked. Browsers don't reveal the full path for security, so only the file name is shown.
export.savedDownload: It's in your browser's downloads folder.
export.memoryFallback: For a large backup, Chrome or Edge is a better fit: you can choose where to save, and data is written to the file as it arrives. This browser builds the whole file first, then downloads it at once.
export.includePhotosHint: Saves the full-size photos from this chat alongside the messages. Stickers are not included. Many photos means much more time and space.
export.includeNote: Attachments that aren't included still appear in the list (attachments.jsonl) with their kind and file id. Only the file itself is missing — the record of what was sent is complete.
export.deviceTitle: This work is done by the device you're on
export.deviceBody: There is no server in the middle, so this PC or phone does all the fetching and compressing itself — which means the time it takes depends heavily on the device. For large chats, or when including photos, use a PC and keep this tab open until it finishes.
export.layoutChat: Right (default) — on the right, as in the app
export.layoutFlat: Left — on the left, so it isn't obvious which side is me
export.layoutHint: Keeping everyone on the left hides which side is you. Your own name is then shown on your messages too.
export.includeStickersHint: Each sticker is stored once no matter how often it was sent. A chat usually has a few dozen kinds, so this stays within a few MB.
export.waitingFor: Waiting {{remain}} for Telegram’s rate limit. It has not stalled — keep this tab open.
export.confirmWholeBody: A busy chat can take hours. This tab must stay open the whole time, and if it is interrupted the download starts over. Splitting it into shorter ranges is safer.
export.confirmDataBody: From here the messages and attachments are actually downloaded. On a mobile network this can use a lot of data. Wi-Fi is safer.
export.confirmAbortBody: What has been downloaded so far will not be saved. Starting again begins from the beginning.
export.anonymizeHint: Names become A, B, C and IDs become 1, 2, 3, with profile photos dropped. The chat name is hidden too. Photos, stickers and other content stay as they are.

errors.title: Error
errors.API_ID_INVALID: The api_id or api_hash is not valid.
errors.API_ID_PUBLISHED_FLOOD: Telegram has restricted this app key. Please register your own api_id at my.telegram.org and try again.
errors.FLOOD_WAIT: Too many requests. Please try again in {{duration}}.
errors.PASSWORD_HASH_INVALID: That two-step verification password is not correct.
errors.PHONE_CODE_EXPIRED: The login code expired. Please request a new one.
errors.PHONE_CODE_INVALID: That login code is not correct.
errors.PHONE_NUMBER_INVALID: That phone number looks wrong. Check that you included the country code.
errors.PHONE_NUMBER_UNOCCUPIED: No Telegram account exists for this number.
errors.PHONE_PASSWORD_FLOOD: Too many password attempts. Please wait and try again.
errors.SESSION_PASSWORD_NEEDED: A two-step verification password is required.
errors.PEER_NOT_CACHED: This chat's details expired. Go back to the list and pick it again.
errors.SESSION_REVOKED: The session was terminated. Please sign in again.
errors.UPDATE_APP_TO_LOGIN: Telegram no longer allows signing in this way.

errorPage.title: Something went wrong while opening this screen
errorPage.body: This may be temporary. Try again, and if it keeps happening, go back to the start.
errorPage.notFoundBody: The address may have changed or been mistyped.
